title: Ensure S3 bucket policy changes are monitored

input_row.title: Ensure S3 bucket policy changes are monitored
input_row.description: Real-time monitoring of API calls can be achieved by directing CloudTrail Logs to CloudWatch Logs or an external Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) environment, and establishing corresponding metric filters and alarms. It is recommended that a metric filter and alarm be established for changes to S3 bucket policies.
input_row.rationale: CloudWatch is an AWS native service that allows you to observe and monitor resources and applications. CloudTrail logs can also be sent to an external Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) environment for monitoring and alerting. Monitoring changes to S3 bucket policies may reduce the time it takes to detect and correct permissive policies on sensitive S3 buckets.
input_row.audit: If you are using CloudTrail trails and CloudWatch, perform the following to ensure that there is at least one active multi-region CloudTrail trail with the prescribed metric filters and alarms configured: 1. Identify the log group name that is configured for use with the active multi-region CloudTrail trail: • List all CloudTrail trails: aws cloudtrail describe-trails • Identify multi-region CloudTrail trails: Trails with "IsMultiRegionTrail" set to true • Note the value associated with "Name":<trail-name> • Note the <trail-log-group-name> within the value associated with "CloudWatchLogsLogGroupArn" o Example: arn:aws:logs:<region>:<account-id>:log- group:<trail-log-group-name>:* • Ensure the identified multi-region CloudTrail trail is active: o aws cloudtrail get-trail-status --name <trail-name> ▪ Ensure IsLogging is set to TRUE • Ensure the identified multi-region CloudTrail trail captures all management events: o aws cloudtrail get-event-selectors --trail-name <trail-name> Internal Only - General ▪ Ensure there is at least one event selector for a trail with IncludeManagementEvents set to true and ReadWriteType set to All 2. Get a list of all associated metric filters for the <trail-log-group-name> captured in step 1: aws logs describe-metric-filters --log-group-name <trail-log-group-name> 3. Ensure the output from the above command contains the following: "filterPattern": "{ ($.eventSource = s3.amazonaws.com) && (($.eventName = PutBucketAcl) || ($.eventName = PutBucketPolicy) || ($.eventName = PutBucketCors) || ($.eventName = PutBucketLifecycle) || ($.eventName = PutBucketReplication) || ($.eventName = DeleteBucketPolicy) || ($.eventName = DeleteBucketCors) || ($.eventName = DeleteBucketLifecycle) || ($.eventName = DeleteBucketReplication)) }" 4. Note the <s3-bucket-policy-changes-metric> value associated with the filterPattern from step 3. 5. Get a list of CloudWatch alarms, and filter on the <s3-bucket-policy-changes-metric> captured in step 4: aws cloudwatch describe-alarms --query 'MetricAlarms[?MetricName==<s3-bucket-policy-changes-metric>]' 6. Note the AlarmActions value; this will provide the SNS topic ARN value. 7. Ensure there is at least one active subscriber to the SNS topic: aws sns list-subscriptions-by-topic --topic-arn <sns-topic-arn> • At least one subscription should have "SubscriptionArn" with a valid AWS ARN. o Example of valid "SubscriptionArn": arn:aws:sns:<region>:<account- id>:<sns-topic-name>:<subscription-id>
input_row.remediation: If you are using CloudTrail trails and CloudWatch, perform the following steps to set up the metric filter, alarm, SNS topic, and subscription: 1. Create a metric filter based on the provided filter pattern that checks for changes to S3 bucket policies and uses the <trail-log-group-name> taken from audit step 1: Internal Only - General aws logs put-metric-filter --log-group-name <trail-log-group-name> --filter-name <s3-bucket-policy-changes-metric> --metric-transformations metricName=<s3-bucket-policy-changes-metric>,metricNamespace='CISBenchmark',metricValue=1 --filter-pattern '{ ($.eventSource = s3.amazonaws.com) && (($.eventName = PutBucketAcl) || ($.eventName = PutBucketPolicy) || ($.eventName = PutBucketCors) || ($.eventName = PutBucketLifecycle) || ($.eventName = PutBucketReplication) || ($.eventName = DeleteBucketPolicy) || ($.eventName = DeleteBucketCors) || ($.eventName = DeleteBucketLifecycle) || ($.eventName = DeleteBucketReplication)) }' Note: You can choose your own metricName and metricNamespace strings. Using the same metricNamespace for all Foundations Benchmark metrics will group them together. 2. Create an SNS topic that the alarm will notify: aws sns create-topic --name <sns-topic-name> Note: You can execute this command once and then reuse the same topic for all monitoring alarms. Note: Capture the TopicArn that is displayed when creating the SNS topic in step 2. 3. Create an SNS subscription for the topic created in step 2: aws sns subscribe --topic-arn <sns-topic-arn> --protocol <sns-protocol> --notification-endpoint <sns-subscription-endpoints> Note: You can execute this command once and then reuse the same subscription for all monitoring alarms. 4. Create an alarm that is associated with the CloudWatch Logs metric filter created in step 1 and the SNS topic created in step 2: aws cloudwatch put-metric-alarm --alarm-name <s3-bucket-policy-changes-alarm> --metric-name <s3-bucket-policy-changes-metric> --statistic Sum --period 300 --threshold 1 --comparison-operator GreaterThanOrEqualToThreshold --evaluation-periods 1 --namespace 'CISBenchmark' --alarm-actions <sns-topic-arn>
input_row.references: 1. CCE-79193-9 2. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awscloudtrail/latest/userguide/cloudwatch-alarms- for-cloudtrail.html 3. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awscloudtrail/latest/userguide/receive-cloudtrail- log-files-from-multiple-regions.html 4. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sns/latest/dg/SubscribeTopic.html Internal Only - General
input_row.additional_information: Configuring a log metric filter and alarm on a multi-region (global) CloudTrail trail: • ensures that activities from all regions (both used and unused) are monitored • ensures that activities on all supported global services are monitored • ensures that all management events across all regions are monitored

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The audit process can fully leverage AWS APIs and is documented with specific AWS CLI commands and boto3 methods to verify the configuration.
- The control specifically details how to audit and remediate configuration settings, including metric filters and CloudTrail logs, which are accessible programmatically.
  
**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_cloudtrail_logs_s3_bucket_policy_changes_monitoring

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
1. **Identify Active Multi-Region CloudTrail Trail:**
   - Use `boto3.client('cloudtrail').describe_trails()` or `aws cloudtrail describe-trails` to list CloudTrail trails.
   - Filter for trails where `"IsMultiRegionTrail"` is `true`.

2. **Verify Trail Status:**
   - Ensure the identified multi-region trail is active using `boto3.client('cloudtrail').get_trail_status(Name=<trail_name>)` or `aws cloudtrail get-trail-status --name <trail-name>`, checking that `IsLogging` is `TRUE`.

3. **Check Event Selectors:**
   - Fetch event selectors with `boto3.client('cloudtrail').get_event_selectors(TrailName=<trail_name>)` or `aws cloudtrail get-event-selectors --trail-name <trail-name>`.
   - Confirm at least one event selector has `IncludeManagementEvents` set to `true` and `ReadWriteType` set to `All`.

4. **List Metric Filters:**
   - Retrieve existing metric filters using `boto3.client('logs').describe_metric_filters(logGroupName=<trail_log_group_name>)` or `aws logs describe-metric-filters --log-group-name <trail-log-group-name>`.
   - Look for the specific metric filter pattern as described.

5. **Check Alarms:**
   - Use `boto3.client('cloudwatch').describe_alarms()` or `aws cloudwatch describe-alarms` and filter for the metric related to S3 bucket policy changes.

6. **SNS Subscriptions:**
   - Identify SNS subscriptions with `boto3.client('sns').list_subscriptions_by_topic(TopicArn=<sns_topic_arn>)` or `aws sns list-subscriptions-by-topic --topic-arn <sns-topic-arn>`.
   - Ensure at least one subscription exists with a valid `SubscriptionArn`.

**Pass/Fail Criteria:**
- **Pass:** The CloudTrail trail is active, logging is enabled, management events are captured, the metric filter for S3 policy changes exists, the corresponding CloudWatch alarm is set up, and the SNS topic is configured with at least one active subscriber.
- **Fail:** If any of the above criteria are not met, further investigation and remediation actions would be warranted.
step2_review:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE

- The classification of the audit approach as Automated is correct. The control can be fully assessed using AWS APIs, boto3, and AWS CLI. The use of CloudTrail, CloudWatch Logs, and SNS can be programmatically verified, making this an appropriate candidate for automation.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 9

- The automation details are technically accurate. The boto3 methods and CLI commands listed are valid and correctly used to assess the configuration of CloudTrail, CloudWatch Logs, and SNS.
- The program name, `aws_cloudtrail_logs_s3_bucket_policy_changes_monitoring`, is descriptive and follows a logical naming convention, though it could be slightly more specific regarding the metric filter and alarm aspect.
- The steps are comprehensive and cover all necessary checks for the control.

**IMPROVEMENTS:**

1. **Program Name Enhancement:**
   - Consider renaming the program to `aws_cloudtrail_s3_bucket_policy_changes_monitoring` to emphasize the focus on S3 bucket policy changes specifically.

2. **Additional Considerations:**
   - Ensure that the metric filter pattern specifically matches the API calls related to S3 bucket policy changes, such as `PutBucketPolicy`, `DeleteBucketPolicy`, etc.
   - Verify that the CloudWatch alarm is configured with an appropriate threshold and period to ensure timely alerts.
   - Consider including a step to verify the SNS topic's policy to ensure it allows the necessary permissions for CloudWatch to publish messages.

3. **Alternative Methods:**
   - Utilize AWS Config rules to continuously monitor the compliance of S3 bucket policies, which can complement the real-time monitoring approach.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

- The assessment is well-structured and technically sound. The use of AWS services and APIs is appropriate and aligns with AWS security best practices.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:** Keep as-is

- The assessment is accurate and comprehensive. The suggested improvements are minor and do not necessitate changes to the overall approach. The audit can proceed with the current automated methodology, ensuring that the additional considerations are reviewed and potentially integrated into the process.
